summary: Provision new Step Functions state machines. Greenfield today (no state machine exists yet in this estate) — the create stands up the execution role, type, tracing and logging shell; the Amazon States Language workflow definition is engineer-authored.

operations.title: Provision a new Step Functions state machine
operations.summary: Request a brand-new state machine; an engineer authors the workflow definition and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a state machine as a complete specification: execution role, workflow type, tracing and logging. The Amazon States Language definition itself is authored by an engineer. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: state_machine_name
params.label: State machine name
params.help: Up to 80 characters. Names the state machine in the Step Functions console and the generated draft.

params.name: definition
params.label: Workflow definition (Amazon States Language)
params.help: The state machine's JSON workflow — states, transitions and error handling — is authored and reviewed by an engineer, never typed into this form.

params.name: execution_role
params.label: Execution role
params.help: The IAM role Step Functions assumes to invoke the workflow's tasks.

params.name: type
params.label: Workflow type
params.help: STANDARD supports long-running/auditable workflows; EXPRESS is for high-volume, short-duration workloads billed per invocation.

params.name: publish
params.label: Publish a version on creation
params.help: Creates an immutable published version alongside the $LATEST alias.

params.name: tracing_enabled
params.label: X-Ray tracing
params.help: Sends trace data for this workflow's executions to AWS X-Ray.

params.name: logging_level
params.label: Logging level
params.help: ALL logs every state transition — high volume, useful while building a workflow.

params.name: logging_include_execution_data
params.label: Include execution data in logs
params.help: Includes state input/output payloads in the log — may contain sensitive workload data.

params.name: log_destination
params.label: CloudWatch log group
params.help: Destination for the execution log, required once logging is turned on.

params.name: encryption_type
params.label: Encryption type
params.help: AWS_OWNED_KEY needs no key selection; CUSTOMER_MANAGED_KMS_KEY uses the KMS key above.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this workflow does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this state machine.

decisions: The Amazon States Language definition is authored and reviewed by an engineer, never captured or validated from this form.
kms_data_key_reuse_period_seconds and any Express workflow log destinations beyond a single CloudWatch log group are engineer follow-ups.
